findings.issue: zh surface adds full Chinese name '超文本标记语言（HTML）' while jp surface is just 'HTML' and en surface is just 'HTML'. Zh surface is more informative but inconsistent with jp/en surface format.

タグ: bracket notation preserved correctly in all 3 langs

surface.jp spells 'EXtensible' with capital E — the accepted expansion is 'eXtensible Markup Language' (capital X only). This non-standard capitalization appears in jp source material and is reproduced as-is.
findings.evidence: jp surface: XML（EXtensible Markup Language）; standard: eXtensible Markup Language

findings.issue: Within the same entity: jp surface uses 'EXtensible' (non-standard) while en surface uses 'Extensible' (standard). Inconsistency between jp and en surface fields.
findings.evidence: jp: XML（EXtensible Markup Language）; en: XML (Extensible Markup Language)

findings.issue: XML definition: contrast with HTML, extensibility concept, use-case distinction all preserved accurately
CSS definition: design/layout purpose preserved accurately in all 3 langs

zh surface '层叠样式表（CSS）' omits the English expansion 'Cascading Style Sheets' that jp and en surfaces include. Consistent with HTML pattern (zh adds native name, drops English expansion) but format is inconsistent within the chunk.
findings.evidence: jp: CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) | zh: 层叠样式表（CSS） | en: CSS (Cascading Style Sheets)

Q1: Manually traced: x=2,y=3→y=2,x=4→y=1,x=5→END. answer_index=1 (x=5) is mathematically correct.

findings.issue: Q2: answer_index=2 (ウ) is domain-correct. All 3 wrong distractors correctly portray false statements about HTML. zh/en translations preserve distractor semantics accurately.
findings.evidence: ア:FTP wrong(HTTP needed)✓; イ:HTML→SGML reversal wrong✓; ウ:CORRECT✓; エ:XML→HTML chronology wrong✓

findings.issue: XML 'EXtensible' non-standard capitalization is consistent between page entity and glossary entry — reproduced faithfully from source material. Not a pipeline error.

summary: PASS — No CRITICAL or MAJOR defects. 20 atomic leaves across 10 pages (pp.332-341, sections 09-04/05/06). 6 MINOR findings: 2 figure caption article/case issues (en), 2 surface format inconsistencies (zh adds full native name for HTML/CSS, omitting English expansion), 1 XML surface capitalization inconsistency between jp ('EXtensible') and en ('Extensible') within entity, 1 glossary-confirmed source-faithful reproduction of 'EXtensible' non-standard cap. All 3 exam questions have verified-correct answer indices. All 8 glossary lookups matched with consistent surface translations.
